question.text: Under Phase II of online grievance processing for RTAs, what key development was mandated from July 1, 2024?

answer.text: Each RTA must develop its own mobile application for grievance filing
All RTAs must integrate their systems with the SEBI SCORES platform directly
RTAs must appoint dedicated grievance officers in every district of India
Qualified RTAs (QRTAs) must establish a common website for online grievance processing

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The online processing framework was rolled out in two phases. Phase I required each RTA to have a functional website with RTA information and an online mechanism for grievance filing. Phase II, effective from July 1, 2024, mandated that Qualified RTAs (QRTAs) establish a common website for streamlined online grievance processing, creating a unified platform for investors.
**The Trap:** Phase II is specifically about a "common website by QRTAs" — not individual mobile apps, direct SCORES integration, or physical office requirements. The concept of a shared platform among QRTAs is unique and tests whether candidates understand the phased implementation approach.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Phased implementation of regulatory requirements is a common approach by SEBI, seen in KYC centralization, T+1 settlement rollout, and now online grievance processing. Understanding the timeline and scope of each phase is frequently tested in NISM exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If your organization is a QRTA, ensure your technology team is aligned with the common website specifications. If you are a smaller RTA, monitor the common website framework as it may eventually extend to all RTAs. Either way, ensure your internal grievance tracking system can interface with the common platform.
